description.overview: Despite the name, Australian Shepherds were developed in the American West. They're intelligent, work-oriented dogs with beautiful coats and often striking blue or multicolored eyes.
description.temperament: Aussies are extremely smart and eager to please. They need jobs to do or they'll create their own. May try to herd children and pets.
description.health: Prone to hip dysplasia, epilepsy, and eye problems. Merle-to-merle breeding can cause deafness and blindness.
description.exercise: Very high exercise and mental stimulation needs. Excel at agility, herding trials, and dog sports.

verdict.summary: Brilliant, athletic dogs that need extensive exercise and mental challenges. Perfect for active owners interested in dog sports. Will become destructive if bored.
